recipes.title: Hāngī
recipes.description: Traditional Māori meal of meat and root vegetables steam-cooked underground over heated stones.
steps: Dig a pit roughly 50 cm deep and build a fire to heat river stones until glowing red.
Rake out the embers, line the pit with the hot stones and cover with a layer of wet sacks.
Season the meats with salt and place into wire baskets with the kūmara, potato and pumpkin chunks.
Lay cabbage leaves over the food, lower the baskets onto the stones and pour over the water for steam.
Cover with more wet sacks then mound earth over the top to seal the pit completely.
Steam undisturbed for three to four hours until the meat is falling apart tender.
Carefully unearth the hāngī, lift out the baskets and serve everything family style.

recipes.title: Pavlova
recipes.description: Crisp meringue dessert with marshmallow centre, claimed by both New Zealand and Australia.
steps: Whisk egg whites until soft peaks form, then add sugar a tablespoon at a time.
Continue whisking until the meringue is thick, glossy and fully dissolved.
Fold in vinegar, cornflour and vanilla until just combined.
Mound onto a lined tray in a 20 cm circle with a slight dip in the centre.
Bake at 120 C for one hour, then turn off the oven and cool inside for two hours.
Whip cream to soft peaks and pile generously into the centre.
Top with sliced kiwifruit and feijoa and serve at once.

recipes.title: Kiwi Burger
recipes.description: New Zealand burger featuring beef patty, fried egg, beetroot slice and salad on a soft bun.
steps: Shape the seasoned mince into four flat patties slightly larger than the buns.
Heat a heavy pan and cook the patties for four minutes per side until well browned.
Lay a slice of cheddar on each patty in the last minute to melt.
In the same pan, fry an egg per burger until the white is just set.
Toast the cut sides of the buns until golden.
Spread tomato sauce on each base, then layer lettuce, tomato slice, patty, beetroot and fried egg.
Cap with the bun lid and press gently to serve.

recipes.title: Lolly Cake
recipes.description: No-bake New Zealand log cake of crushed Malt biscuits, condensed milk and Eskimo lollies, rolled in coconut.
steps: Crush the malt biscuits into fine crumbs in a large bowl.
Snip the Eskimo lollies into small pieces and add to the crumbs.
Melt butter with the condensed milk in a saucepan over low heat until smooth.
Pour the warm mixture over the dry ingredients and stir to a sticky dough.
Tip the mixture onto a board sprinkled with desiccated coconut.
Shape into a long log about 5 cm thick and roll fully in the coconut to coat.
Wrap in baking paper and chill for at least three hours before slicing.

recipes.title: Whitebait Fritter
recipes.description: Light egg fritters bound with tiny whole West Coast whitebait, served with lemon.
steps: Rinse the whitebait gently in cold water and drain well.
Lightly whisk eggs with salt and white pepper, do not overbeat.
Stir the whitebait through the eggs.
Melt half the butter in a hot pan and ladle in a quarter of the mixture.
Cook for one minute until set underneath, then flip and cook for 30 seconds more.
Repeat with the remaining mixture, adding more butter as needed.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges and buttered white bread.

recipes.title: Afghan Biscuit
recipes.description: Chocolate biscuit topped with chocolate icing and a single walnut half, a New Zealand bakery classic.
steps: Cream butter and caster sugar until pale and fluffy.
Sift in flour and cocoa, then fold through the cornflakes carefully so they stay mostly intact.
Place tablespoonfuls of mixture onto lined trays and press down lightly.
Bake at 180 C for 15 minutes, then cool fully on the trays.
Sift icing sugar with extra cocoa, whisk in boiling water to a thick spreadable icing.
Spread a teaspoon of icing onto each biscuit.
Top each with a walnut half before the icing sets.

recipes.title: Hokey Pokey Ice Cream
recipes.description: Vanilla ice cream studded with crunchy honeycomb toffee, New Zealand's most loved flavour.
steps: Combine sugar and golden syrup in a saucepan and stir over low heat until dissolved.
Bring to a boil and cook without stirring for five minutes until deep amber.
Whisk in the bicarbonate of soda quickly, the mixture will foam dramatically.
Pour onto a lined tray and leave to set hard, then smash into small shards.
Whip the cream to soft peaks, then fold through condensed milk and vanilla.
Fold most of the honeycomb shards through the cream mixture.
Pour into a freezer container, scatter the rest of the honeycomb over the top and freeze overnight.

recipes.title: Boil-up
recipes.description: Hearty Māori one pot of pork bones simmered with watercress, kūmara and dough boys.
steps: Place pork bones in a large pot and cover with cold water, bring to a boil and skim the foam.
Reduce to a gentle simmer, season with salt and pepper and cook for 90 minutes.
Add chunked kūmara and potato and continue simmering for 20 minutes.
Mix self raising flour with water and a pinch of salt to a soft dough.
Drop tablespoonfuls of dough onto the simmering broth to make doughboys.
Cover and cook for 15 minutes until the doughboys are puffed and cooked through.
Stir through the chopped watercress just before serving so it wilts in the broth.

recipes.title: Pikelet
recipes.description: Small thick pancakes served warm with butter and jam, a New Zealand morning tea favourite.
steps: Whisk flour, sugar and salt in a bowl, making a well in the centre.
Beat egg with milk and pour into the well, whisking to a smooth thick batter.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and brush with melted butter.
Drop tablespoons of batter into the pan, leaving room to spread.
Cook for 90 seconds until bubbles form on the surface, then flip.
Cook the second side for one minute until golden.
Stack on a tea towel to keep warm and serve with butter and jam.
